text:
Once upon a time, in a small village nestled between sparkling rivers and deep forests, there lived a curious old owl named Oliver. Unlike his cautious neighbors, Oliver was always scanning the skies, searching for adventures far beyond their sleepy rut.

One crisp autumn evening, Oliver spotted a flicker in the treetops. It was a magnificent banner fluttering with hues of orange and lavender. Intrigued, he hooted to his tiny flock.

"Look!" Oliver whispered. "A kite made of flowers! Maybe it's not just a trick!"

He darted from branch to branch until he reached the spot. The kite soared gracefully, dancing to the breeze. Oliver couldn't resist a chase. He soared after it for hours, dodging leaves and twigs. Finally, the kite fluttered down into a warm hedge near the pond.

Creeping closer, Oliver saw it was woven with the finest hairs from unicorns. The knots formed patterns like tiny flowers.
